comments.body:
@cevich PTAL — This is the first GitHub action update, and I’m not quite sure how much scrutiny we need with the digest pins.

(Also, the release notes suggest some config file and behavior changes: https://github.com/actions/stale/compare/98ed4cb500039dbcccf4bd9bedada4d0187f2757...65b52aff67b46a817f078f5b004787c9759ceac9 )

>  I’m not quite sure how much scrutiny we need with the digest pins.

It's a **feature** :disappointed: 

Github security recommends pinning to digests instead of version numbers because some evil-doer could re-tag their evil commit awakening SkyNet.

I think it was a mistake to move to digests, they're not human-friendly and the risk (for an action OWNED by github) is so low it's not worth considering.  I'll see about switching back to semantic versions and adding a monthly-update policy for GHA digest pins (if there are any).

\*shrug\* I’m fine with digests in principle, I’m primarily asking what should I be reviewing.

Maybe actually reading the code is not important, but we should ensure that the digest indeed exists on the upstream branch?

> I’m primarily asking what should I be reviewing.

Oh, please don't spend time on these.  Just assign them to me, I'll take care of 'em.

> Maybe actually reading the code is not important, but we should ensure that the digest indeed exists on the upstream branch?

No we should probably switch to using the tagged versions.  Glancing at the changes is important, and (IMHO) easier with tags than digests.  I've run into at least on instance where there was a breaking change that needed some tweaks.

Renovate is now listing a failure on https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1782 , 
the logs seem to suggest this dependency:
```
DEBUG: Value mismatch(packageFile=".github/workflows/stale.yml", branch="renovate/actions-stale-digest")
{
  "manager": "github-actions",
  "foundValue": "v3"
}
WARN: Error updating branch: update failure(branch="renovate/actions-stale-digest")
```

Is it parsing the _comment_?
Renovate relies pretty heavily on regular expressions, so my guess is one is more "loose" than it should be.  And we should be using the versions anyway, so -shrug-
